description:
You are acting as a cloud-migration architect who must perform a batch hostname migration and create a reproducible audit trail.  
All work must be done with normal user privileges under /home/user.

Background  
• The monorepo is rooted at /home/user/projects/microservices.  
• Every service directory under that root contains a sub-directory called config/ holding one or more “*.conf” files.  
• Each “*.conf” file may contain the legacy hostname legacy.internal.example.com one or more times.  
• Every service directory also contains a sub-directory called scripts/ that holds assorted “*.sh” helper scripts.  
• A migration backup directory does NOT yet exist.

Required end state  
1. Replace every occurrence of legacy.internal.example.com with cloud.internal.example.com inside every “*.conf” file found anywhere below /home/user/projects/microservices.  
   • Use a combination of find and xargs (or find … -exec … \+) to perform the edits in batch.  
   • After the edit, no remaining line in any “*.conf” file may contain legacy.internal.example.com.  
2. Generate a log file called /home/user/migration_logs/hostname_migration.log whose content records exactly which files were modified and the number of textual replacements performed in each file.  
   • The log directory /home/user/migration_logs must be created if it does not already exist.  
   • The log file must be UTF-8 plain text.  
   • Each modified file must be represented by exactly one line in this log.  
   • The log must use JSON-per-line (a.k.a. “JSON Lines”) format. The required schema for every line is:  
     {"file":"<absolute_path>","replaced":<integer>}  
   • Lines must be sorted in lexicographical order by the absolute file path.  
   • An example of *one* correct line (do **NOT** hard-code this path) is:  
     {"file":"/home/user/projects/microservices/serviceZ/config/api.conf","replaced":3}  
3. Create a backup directory tree that gathers every “*.sh” file which has a filesystem modification date not older than 90 days counted from the current system date when the script runs.  
   • The destination directory must be /home/user/migration_backup/scripts (create any missing parent directories).  
   • For each qualifying “*.sh” file, copy it into the backup directory **while preserving its path relative to /home/user/projects/microservices**.  
     Example: /home/user/projects/microservices/serviceA/scripts/deploy.sh must end up at  
     /home/user/migration_backup/scripts/serviceA/scripts/deploy.sh  
   • Files older than 90 days must NOT be copied.  
   • Use find together with xargs (or find … -exec … \+) so the copy runs in one batch command, not individual manual cp calls.  
4. Produce on-screen confirmation that summarises:  
   • Total number of “*.conf” files modified.  
   • Aggregate number of legacy.internal.example.com occurrences replaced across all files.  
   • Total number of “*.sh” files copied into the backup.  
   (Exact wording is up to you; the information simply needs to be echoed to STDOUT.)

File & directory ownership/permissions  
• Everything under /home/user is owned by the current unprivileged user.  
• The script must not invoke sudo.

Important notes for automatic grading  
• The grader will examine:  
  – The contents of every “*.conf” file to confirm replacement.  
  – The exact JSON-Lines contents of /home/user/migration_logs/hostname_migration.log.  
  – That the log file paths are absolute and sorted.  
  – The presence and correct relative placement of the copied “*.sh” scripts under /home/user/migration_backup/scripts.  
• Do NOT hard-code counts/filenames; discover them via the filesystem.  
• Use only POSIX-compliant shell tools available in a minimal Debian/Ubuntu container (find, grep, sed, awk, xargs, cp, echo, mkdir, date, stat, etc.).  
• The whole task must be achievable with a handful of well-composed terminal commands; no interactive text editors may be used.

Outcome  
When you have finished, the migration is complete, the audit log exists with the specified format, and the backup directory tree contains the recent scripts.
truth:
Initial filesystem layout (before the agent starts)
---------------------------------------------------
/home/user/projects/microservices/serviceA/config/app.conf
    line1: DB_HOST=legacy.internal.example.com
    line2: CACHE_HOST=legacy.internal.example.com
/home/user/projects/microservices/serviceA/config/db.conf
    line1: jdbc=postgres://legacy.internal.example.com:5432/prod
/home/user/projects/microservices/serviceB/config/web.conf
    line1: upstream legacy.internal.example.com;
/home/user/projects/microservices/serviceB/config/cache.conf
    line1: cache_server=legacy.internal.example.com
    line2: metrics_host=legacy.internal.example.com
    line3: backup_host=legacy.internal.example.com
/home/user/projects/microservices/serviceC/config/worker.conf
    line1: queue=amqp://legacy.internal.example.com

/home/user/projects/microservices/serviceA/scripts/deploy.sh   (mtime: 30 days ago)
/home/user/projects/microservices/serviceB/scripts/restart.sh  (mtime: 20 days ago)
/home/user/projects/microservices/serviceC/scripts/cleanup.sh  (mtime: 91 days ago)

No directory called /home/user/migration_logs exists yet.
No directory called /home/user/migration_backup exists yet.

Ground-truth expected after correct completion
----------------------------------------------
All six occurrences of legacy.internal.example.com in *.conf files are replaced by cloud.internal.example.com.

Expected final contents of each modified file:
/home/user/projects/microservices/serviceA/config/app.conf
    DB_HOST=cloud.internal.example.com
    CACHE_HOST=cloud.internal.example.com
/home/user/projects/microservices/serviceA/config/db.conf
    jdbc=postgres://cloud.internal.example.com:5432/prod
/home/user/projects/microservices/serviceB/config/web.conf
    upstream cloud.internal.example.com;
/home/user/projects/microservices/serviceB/config/cache.conf
    cache_server=cloud.internal.example.com
    metrics_host=cloud.internal.example.com
    backup_host=cloud.internal.example.com
/home/user/projects/microservices/serviceC/config/worker.conf
    queue=amqp://cloud.internal.example.com

The file /home/user/migration_logs/hostname_migration.log exists and contains exactly five lines,
sorted lexicographically by path (serviceA before serviceB before serviceC):

{"file":"/home/user/projects/microservices/serviceA/config/app.conf","replaced":2}
{"file":"/home/user/projects/microservices/serviceA/config/db.conf","replaced":1}
{"file":"/home/user/projects/microservices/serviceB/config/cache.conf","replaced":3}
{"file":"/home/user/projects/microservices/serviceB/config/web.conf","replaced":1}
{"file":"/home/user/projects/microservices/serviceC/config/worker.conf","replaced":1}

Total replacements across all files = 8.

Backup directory expectations:
/home/user/migration_backup/scripts/serviceA/scripts/deploy.sh     (copied)
/home/user/migration_backup/scripts/serviceB/scripts/restart.sh    (copied)
/home/user/migration_backup/scripts/serviceC/scripts/cleanup.sh    (NOT copied because older than 90 days)

Thus exactly two *.sh files should exist under /home/user/migration_backup/scripts after completion.

The automated grader will:
1. Check that no *.conf file still contains legacy.internal.example.com.
2. Validate that each *.conf file equals the “Expected final contents” above.
3. Validate that hostname_migration.log exists, is UTF-8, has exactly the five JSON lines shown, in that precise order.
4. Confirm that the two recent *.sh files were copied to the backup directory with preserved relative paths and that cleanup.sh was NOT copied.
5. Confirm that exactly two files exist inside /home/user/migration_backup/scripts/ (the directory structure may contain nested folders).
